result.markdown:
--- title: "BHM Basket Trial Logistic Regression - RStan v JAGS v Nimble" output: rmarkdown::html_vignette: toc: true toc_depth: 2 number_sections: true vignette: > %\VignetteIndexEntry{Two-arm trial TFP v RStan} %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown} %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8} --- ## Quick start This vignette compares the same Bayesian hierarchical model fitted with Rstan, JAGS and Nimble, and is similar the previous vignette for a logistic regression but extened to a basket trial design scenario with two baskets. Only key snippets and outputs are shown below. The full vignette is available at [here](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GSK-Biostatistics/vignettes-asia/refs/heads/main/jags_nimble/stan_jags_nimble2.qmd.qmd). ### Model Formulation The model implemented here is a non-centered parameterization for a Basket trial with two baskets and a logistic response model. $$ \begin{aligned} \mu_0 &\sim \text{Normal}(0, 2.5)\\ \sigma_0 &\sim \text{Half-Normal}(0, 2.5) \\ \mu_1 &\sim \text{Normal}(0, 2.5) \\ \sigma_1 &\sim \text{Half-Normal}(0, 2.5) \\ \tilde{\theta}_j &\sim \text{Normal}(0, 1)\quad\enspace\text{for}\enspace{j=1,\dots,4} \\ \beta_{0,0} &= \mu_0 + \sigma_0 \tilde{\theta}_1 \quad\quad\hspace{0.2cm}\text{ intercept - log odds control basket 0}\\ \beta_{0,1} &= \mu_0 + \sigma_0 \tilde{\theta}_2 \quad\quad\hspace{0.2cm}\text{ intercept - log odds control basket 1}\\ \beta_{1,0} &= \mu_1 + \sigma_1 \tilde{\theta}_3 \quad\quad\hspace{0.2cm}\text{ treatment - log OR basket 0}\\ \beta_{1,1} &= \mu_1 + \sigma_1 \tilde{\theta}_4 \quad\quad\hspace{0.2cm}\text{ treatment - log OR basket 1}\\ \text{logit(}{p_i}) &= \beta_0 + \beta_1 z_i\quad\quad\enspace\enspace\text{for}\enspace{i=1,\dots,N} \\ y_i &\sim \text{Bernoulli}(p_i) \end{aligned} $$ ## Data set The R chunk below creates a simple dataset of three cols: - a binary response variable y (0/1 = non-responder/responder), - a (dummy) basket ID variable (=1) - a binary treatment variable (0/1 = control/test treatment) The basket ID is currently set fixed at 1, denoting there is only one basket in this trial, i.e. a classical two arm randomized trial design. Baskets will be added in other vignettes. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ### Prepare model inputs set.seed(99999) # Set up data rr_k_ctrl <- c(0.20,0.25) # control response rate for each basket rr_k_trt <- c(0.40,0.45) # treatment response rate for each basket K<-length(rr_k_ctrl) # number of baskets N_k_ctrl <- rep(50, K) # number of control participants per basket N_k_trt <- rep(50, K) # number of treatment participants per basket N_k <- N_k_ctrl + N_k_trt # number of participants per basket (both arms combined) N <- sum(N_k) # total sample size k_vec <- rep(1:K, N_k) # N x 1 vector of basket indicators (1 to K) z_vec<-NULL; y<-NULL; for(i in 1:K){ # for each basket repeat 0-control 1-trt according to the specifc Ns z_vec<-c(z_vec,rep(0:1,c(N_k_ctrl[i],N_k_trt[i]))) # treatment/control indicator y<-c(y, c(rbinom(N_k_ctrl[i],1,rr_k_ctrl[i]), # bernoulli for control rbinom(N_k_trt[i],1,rr_k_trt[i]))) # for trt } thedata<-data.frame(y,basketID=k_vec,Treatment=z_vec) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} my_table1_object <- head(thedata) %>% kable() %>% kable_styling(full_width = F) %>% column_spec(1, bold = F)#, color = "blue") #> Warning: 'xfun::attr()' is deprecated. #> Use 'xfun::attr2()' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") #> Warning: 'xfun::attr()' is deprecated. #> Use 'xfun::attr2()' instead. #> See help("Deprecated") my_table1_object ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
y basketID Treatment
0 1 0
1 1 0
0 1 0
1 1 0
0 1 0
0 1 0
````` ::: ::: ## RStan The code for implementing the above model is RStan is given below. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ### Stan model BHM_stan_1 <- "data { int K; // number of baskets (K >= 2) int N; // total number of participants int N_k[K]; // K x 1 vector of basket sample sizes //int N_k; int k_vec[N]; // N x 1 vector of basket indicators int z_vec[N]; // N x 1 vector of treatment indicators for active treatment arm int y[N]; // N x 1 vector of binary responses } parameters { matrix[K,2] beta_tr; // K x 2 matrix of transformed regression coefficients real mu1; // scalar of hierarchical mean (log odds ratio) real sigma1; // scalar of hierarchical SD (log odds ratio) real mu0; // scalar of hierarchical mean (log odds ratio) real sigma0; // scalar of hierarchical SD (log odds ratio) } transformed parameters { matrix[K,2] beta; // K x 2 matrix of regression coefficients (without transformation) // Calculate beta coefs using transformed betas (leads to better mixing) for (k in 1:K){ beta[k,1] = mu0 + sigma0 * beta_tr[k,1]; beta[k,2] = mu1 + sigma1 * beta_tr[k,2]; } } model { mu0 ~ normal(0, 2.5); // vectorized normal priors for hierarchical means (specify SD in normal distn) sigma0 ~ normal(0, 2.5); // vectorized half-normal priors for hierarchical SDs (specify SD in normal distn) //sigma0 ~ exponential(1); //sigma0 ~ cauchy(0, 5); //sigma0 ~ gamma(2, 0.1); mu1 ~ normal(0, 2.5); // vectorized normal priors for hierarchical means (specify SD in normal distn) sigma1 ~ normal(0, 2.5); // vectorized half-normal priors for hierarchical SDs (specify SD in normal distn) //sigma1 ~ exponential(1); //sigma1 ~ cauchy(0, 5); //sigma1 ~ gamma(2, 0.1); beta_tr[,1] ~ normal(0, 1); // vectorized normal prior for the log odds for the control arm beta_tr[,2] ~ normal(0, 1); // vectorized normal prior for the log odds ratios for (i in 1:N) y[i] ~ bernoulli_logit(beta[k_vec[i], 1] + beta[k_vec[i], 2] * z_vec[i]); } " ``` ::: ### Diagnostics ### Summarize results ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(bayesplot) color_scheme_set("viridisA") p<-mcmc_trace(stan_fit_2,"mu0") #ggsave("precomputed/vg2_traceplot1_stan.png", p) print(p) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](stan_jags_nimble2_files/figure-html/stanresults-1.png){width=100%} ::: ```{.r .cell-code} plot(stan_fit_2) #> 'pars' not specified. Showing first 10 parameters by default. #> ci_level: 0.8 (80% intervals) #> outer_level: 0.95 (95% intervals) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](stan_jags_nimble2_files/figure-html/stanresults-2.png){width=100%} ::: ::: ### JAGS ### NIMBLE with default sampler ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nimble_code_1 <- nimbleCode({ for (i in 1:N) { p[i] <- ilogit(beta[k_vec[i], 1] + beta[k_vec[i], 2] * z_vec[i]) y[i] ~ dbern(p[i]) } for (k in 1:K) { beta_tr[k, 1] ~ dnorm(0, 1) beta_tr[k, 2] ~ dnorm(0, 1) } for (j in 1:K){ beta[j,1] <- mu0 + sigma0 * beta_tr[j,1] beta[j,2] <- mu1 + sigma1 * beta_tr[j,2] } #tau0 <- 1 / (sigma0^2) #tau1 <- 1 / (sigma1^2) tau_mu <- 1 / (2.5^2) mu0 ~ dnorm(0, tau_mu) mu1 ~ dnorm(0, tau_mu) sigma0 ~ dunif(0, 3) sigma1 ~ dunif(0, 3) } ) #Initial values K_val <- data_input_1$K N_val <- data_input_1$N inits <- list( mu0 = 0, mu1 = 0, sigma0 = 1, sigma1 = 1, beta_tr = matrix(0, nrow = K_val, ncol = 2), beta = matrix(0, nrow = K_val, ncol = 2) ) # Build model model <- nimbleModel( nimble_code_1, # Ensure k_vec is in constants, not data constants = list( N = N_val, K = K_val, k_vec = data_input_1$k_vec, z_vec = data_input_1$z_vec ), data = list( y = data_input_1$y ), inits = inits, dimensions = list( beta_tr = c(K_val, 2), beta = c(K_val, 2), k_vec = c(N_val) ) ) #> Defining model #> Building model #> Setting data and initial values #> Running calculate on model #> [Note] Any error reports that follow may simply reflect missing values in model variables. #> Checking model sizes and dimensions #Configure the default sampler # NIMBLE automatically detects conjugacy (Gibbs) and assigns # Adaptive Metropolis to non-conjugate parameters. mcmcConf <- configureMCMC(model) mcmcConf$addMonitors("beta") #conf$addMonitors(c("mu0", "mu1", "sigma0", "sigma1", "beta_tr", "beta")) #Build MCMC mcmc <- buildMCMC(mcmcConf) # Compile Cmodel <- compileNimble(model) #> Compiling #> [Note] This may take a minute. #> [Note] Use 'showCompilerOutput = TRUE' to see C++ compilation details. # Build and compile HMC sampler Cmcmc <- compileNimble(mcmc, project = model) #> Compiling #> [Note] This may take a minute. #> [Note] Use 'showCompilerOutput = TRUE' to see C++ compilation details. # Run the sampler samples <- runMCMC( Cmcmc, niter = 20000, nburnin = 5000, thin = 10, nchains = 4, samplesAsCodaMCMC = TRUE ) #> running chain 1... #> running chain 2... #> running chain 3... #> running chain 4... # Diagnosis library(coda) #plot(samples) summary(samples) gelman.diag(samples) effectiveSize(samples) all_samples <- do.call(rbind, samples) beta_nimble1 <- all_samples[, "beta[1, 1]"] ``` ::: ### NIMBLE with HMC ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nimble_code_1 <- nimbleCode({ for (i in 1:N) { p[i] <- ilogit(beta[k_vec[i], 1] + beta[k_vec[i], 2] * z_vec[i]) y[i] ~ dbern(p[i]) } for (k in 1:K) { beta_tr[k, 1] ~ dnorm(0, 1) beta_tr[k, 2] ~ dnorm(0, 1) } for (j in 1:K){ beta[j,1] <- mu0 + sigma0 * beta_tr[j,1] beta[j,2] <- mu1 + sigma1 * beta_tr[j,2] } #tau0 <- 1 / (sigma0^2) #tau1 <- 1 / (sigma1^2) tau_mu <- 1 / (2.5^2) mu0 ~ dnorm(0, tau_mu) mu1 ~ dnorm(0, tau_mu) sigma0 ~ dunif(0, 3) sigma1 ~ dunif(0, 3) } ) #Initial values K_val <- data_input_1$K N_val <- data_input_1$N inits <- list( mu0 = 0, mu1 = 0, sigma0 = 1, sigma1 = 1, beta_tr = matrix(0, nrow = K_val, ncol = 2), beta = matrix(0, nrow = K_val, ncol = 2) ) # Build model model <- nimbleModel( nimble_code_1, # Ensure k_vec is in constants, not data constants = list( N = N_val, K = K_val, k_vec = data_input_1$k_vec, z_vec = data_input_1$z_vec ), data = list( y = data_input_1$y ), inits = inits, dimensions = list( beta_tr = c(K_val, 2), beta = c(K_val, 2), k_vec = c(N_val) ), buildDerivs = TRUE ) #> Defining model #> Building model #> Setting data and initial values #> Running calculate on model #> [Note] Any error reports that follow may simply reflect missing values in model variables. #> Checking model sizes and dimensions # Compile Cmodel <- compileNimble(model) #> Compiling #> [Note] This may take a minute. #> [Note] Use 'showCompilerOutput = TRUE' to see C++ compilation details. # Key step: Configure HMC conf <- configureHMC(model) conf$addMonitors(c("mu0", "mu1", "sigma0", "sigma1", "beta_tr", "beta")) conf$printSamplers() # Build and compile HMC sampler Rmcmc <- buildMCMC(conf) Cmcmc <- compileNimble(Rmcmc, project = model) #> Compiling #> [Note] This may take a minute. #> [Note] Use 'showCompilerOutput = TRUE' to see C++ compilation details. # Run the sampler samples <- runMCMC( Cmcmc, niter = 20000, nburnin = 5000, thin = 10, nchains = 4, samplesAsCodaMCMC = TRUE ) #> running chain 1... #> running chain 2... #> running chain 3... #> running chain 4... # Diagnosis library(coda) #plot(samples) summary(samples) gelman.diag(samples) effectiveSize(samples) all_samples <- do.call(rbind, samples) beta_nimble2 <- all_samples[, "beta[1, 1]"] ``` ::: ### Plot and compare ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} df <- data.frame( value = c(beta_stan, beta_jags, beta_nimble1, beta_nimble2), source = factor(rep( c("Stan", "JAGS", "NIMBLE", "NIMBLE - HMC"), times = c(length(beta_stan), length(beta_jags), length(beta_nimble1), length(beta_nimble2)) )) ) library(ggplot2) ggplot(df, aes(x = value, color = source)) + geom_density(size = 1) + labs(title = "Posterior density of beta[1,1]", x = "beta[1,1]", y = "Density") + theme_minimal() #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](stan_jags_nimble2_files/figure-html/plot_res, plot_res-1.png){width=672} ::: :::
